text.title: Design Doc Advanced Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Doc Advanced Patterns. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: When a trigger fires (cross-system, hot-path, security, migration) the matching block is mandatory — not optional.
rule.rationale: Optional advanced blocks get skipped under deadline pressure; trigger-driven mandates prevent that.

rule.statement: Sequence diagrams name every actor; anonymous boxes ('service A', 'frontend') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Anonymous boxes prevent the reviewer from tracing the change against the real architecture.

rule.statement: Capacity claims cite a source (load test, p95 metric, vendor SLO); 'should be fast' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Unsourced capacity claims are guesses; CI cannot regression-check them.

rule.statement: Failure-mode table carries an MTTR column; rows without MTTR are rejected.
rule.rationale: MTTR is the metric that ranks failure modes by recovery cost.

rule.statement: Migration playbook specifies a rollback budget in minutes that fits the ops SLO.
rule.rationale: Without a budget, mid-migration rollback decisions become panic-driven.

rule.statement: Every advanced block links back to the matching section in the base design doc.
rule.rationale: Free-floating advanced blocks become orphaned references during reviews.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-doc-advanced-patterns.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
